admin_panel.notes: The password is the Wi-Fi password on the sticker on the bottom of the modem.

ethernet.pppoe_clear_note: If you've been using this modem with another internet provider, the old settings may still be saved. Change 'Connection type / Authentication mode' to 'IPoE / DHCP / Dynamic' and clear any username and password fields.
steps_ipoe: Connect an Ethernet cable from the nbn connection box (UNI-D1 port) to the RED WAN port on the back of the modem
If UNI-D1 doesn't work, try the other UNI-D ports on the nbn box
Plug in the power cable and turn on the modem
Wait 2-5 minutes for the modem to boot — the WAN light should turn solid green
Connect a device to the modem via Ethernet or Wi-Fi (credentials on the bottom sticker)
Open a web browser and go to http://192.168.0.1 or http://mygateway/
Log in with username 'admin' and the Wi-Fi password from the bottom sticker
Navigate to the 'Internet Access' or broadband settings section
Ensure the connection type is set to 'IPoE' or 'DHCP' or 'Dynamic' (NOT PPPoE)
Clear any username/password fields if present
Click Save — the modem should reconnect within 1-2 minutes

dsl.pppoe_clear_note: If you've been using this modem with another internet provider, the old settings may still be saved. Change 'Connection type / Authentication mode' to 'IPoE / DHCP / Dynamic' and clear any username and password fields.
steps_ipoe: Connect a phone cable from the wall socket to the grey DSL port on the back of the modem
Plug in the power cable and turn on the modem
Wait 2-5 minutes for the DSL to sync — the WAN/DSL light should turn solid green
Connect a device to the modem via Ethernet or Wi-Fi (credentials on the bottom sticker)
Open a web browser and go to http://192.168.0.1 or http://mygateway/
Log in with username 'admin' and the Wi-Fi password from the bottom sticker
Navigate to the 'Internet Access' or broadband settings section
Ensure the connection type is set to 'IPoE' or 'DHCP' or 'Dynamic' (NOT PPPoE)
Clear any username/password fields if present
Click Save — the modem should reconnect within 1-2 minutes

wan_config.vlan_notes: VLAN settings are NOT accessible in the standard Telstra firmware UI. This is fine for Belong (no VLAN needed). ISPs that require VLAN tagging (e.g. TPG with VLAN 2) cannot use this modem without rooting the firmware.

physical.wan_port_position: Red Ethernet port on rear panel
physical.dsl_port_position: Grey RJ11 port on rear panel

factory_reset.method: Use a pin or paperclip to press and hold the recessed Reset button on the rear panel for about 10 seconds until the lights start flashing. The modem will reboot to factory defaults.
factory_reset.notes: Factory reset restores the admin password to the Wi-Fi password printed on the modem's bottom sticker. It also clears any ISP configuration. Recommended as the first step when repurposing a Telstra modem for Belong — it removes any old Telstra-specific settings.

firmware.check_path: Admin panel — firmware section (exact path varies by firmware version)
firmware.notes: Telstra controls firmware updates for this device — there are no public firmware downloads. The modem may auto-update when connected to Telstra's network, but will NOT receive firmware updates when used with another ISP. This is a minor concern but means the device may eventually fall behind on security patches.

setup.setup_notes: The Telstra Smart Modem Gen 3 has a custom Telstra firmware UI that differs from the base Technicolor interface. The admin UI is simpler than stock Technicolor but also more limited — notably, VLAN settings are hidden. For Belong, this doesn't matter (no VLAN needed). The key gotcha is the ISP_LOCK condition: while Telstra officially says the modem is locked, community consensus (Whirlpool) confirms it works fine with IPoE ISPs like Belong. The lock primarily affects VoIP features and VLAN access, not basic internet connectivity. Factory reset is strongly recommended before configuring for Belong to clear any old Telstra settings. The default admin password is unique per unit (printed on sticker), not a generic 'admin' or 'Telstra' — this is a common point of confusion.

setup_sources.description: Swoop — Telstra Gen 3 setup guide for HFC/FTTP/FTTC (IPoE and PPPoE steps, port identification)
Swoop — Telstra Gen 3 setup guide for FTTN/B (DSL port, phone cable, sync steps)
Whirlpool — Telstra Smart Modem Gen 3 ISP lock discussion (confirmed works with IPoE ISPs)
Upstream Tech — Configuring Telstra Smart Modem for NBN (admin access, broadband tab, factory reset)
Whirlpool — Configuring Telstra Smart Modem for different ISP (IPoE to PPPoE switching)

setup_confidence.notes: Admin IP (192.168.0.1 / mygateway) confirmed by Telstra, Swoop, and Upstream Tech. Default credentials (admin / sticker password) confirmed by multiple sources. Physical ports (red WAN, grey DSL) confirmed by Swoop guides. ISP lock nuance (works with IPoE despite 'locked' label) confirmed by Whirlpool community. However: exact admin UI menu path to WAN/broadband settings is inconsistently documented across sources — described as 'broadband tab' or 'Internet Access' but no source provides the precise click-by-click path with screenshots (-15). Telstra's custom firmware UI varies between firmware versions and no public documentation exists (-10). IPoE label in the UI unconfirmed — may be 'IPoE', 'DHCP', or 'Dynamic' depending on firmware version (-10). Score: 100 - 15 - 10 - 10 = 65.
